next_step_text: 6/29- ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM with the following due dates. Intent to Respond Due: 06/09/2026 RFP Questions Due: 06/16/2026 Proposal Submission Deadline: 07/10/2026 6/1 - No material Update, Michaels efforts are finally paying off. He has convinced management to kick off an RFP for CLM ands Zycus will be included. He will share Project timeline and next steps once final approval ha

recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Received] Zycus: RFP – Consolidated Responses to Submitted Questions
[Clari - Email Sent] Re: Zycus: RFP – Consolidated Responses to Submitted Questions

buyer_dependent_items.text: Share the post-proposal selection and award timeline after the 10 Jul submission

competitive_position.summary: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest current threat: the director-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to validate, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin. Do-nothing/enhance-Conga is the active fallback to beat on differentiation and bundled price. Coupa is dormant, surfacing only as Gartner peer-positioning. The field is now a formal multi-supplier RFP, so proposal quality and price decide it.

competitors.name: Conga (Salesforce CLM)
competitors.quote: their vision is still the Conga is a repository. In my opinion, it's not a very good one, but it is a repository
competitors.how_we_win: Lead the RFP response with the gap set Conga cannot close (external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI contract discovery and risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, D365/Merlin) plus a bundled TCO exposing the hidden cost of enhancing Conga with separate compare/sign tools.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / enhance Conga in-place
competitors.quote: can I get the AI module without buying anything else
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor replacement value against the hidden cost of enhancing Conga plus point tools; make the totality-of-solution TCO the centerpiece.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Catch up with Zycus 09 Jan 2026 (peer-positioning context)
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active bidder; reinforce the Gartner Magic Quadrant peer positioning Michael already values.

champion_strength.source: Zycus Debrief 16 Oct 2025; Catch up with Zycus 09 Jan 2026; SF Next_Step 6/29
champion_strength.summary: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to the skeptical budget owner and admits no prior Zycus experience. Making him the economic-buyer broker with a quantified value narrative is the highest-leverage use of his strength.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with a director referred to on calls only as 'mister Bevan' who mandated the Conga gap analysis and, per Michael, questioned buying the AI module standalone ('can I get the AI module without buying anything else' - 16 Oct 2025). He has never appeared on a Zycus call and is not in the Salesforce contact roles, so his exact name and title are unconfirmed from the relevant stakeholder this champion-authored tender the path to him runs THROUGH Michael Tutton, not around him: arming the champion with a leadership-facing value and ROI narrative is the move, not cold outreach to an unverified contact.

recommended_moves: Submit the CLM RFP proposal by 10 Jul leading with the gaps Conga cannot close.
Anchor a bundled iContract, iSupplier and Merlin TCO in the proposal to answer the module-cost concern.
Equip Michael Tutton with a one-page quantified ROI narrative to carry to the budget owner.
